skill.name: video
skill.description: Guides AI agents through video production workflows, including AI video generation, avatar videos, programmatic rendering, and content repurposing.
skill.summary: Create practical AI-assisted video production plans, prompts, and workflows for marketing videos.
skill.author: coreyhaines31

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk matches, but review found they are false positives from markdown text, tool names, code fences, and relative documentation links. The skill contains no executable script files and no confirmed malicious intent. Low-risk concerns remain because it suggests reading local product context, references external services, and shows package installation examples.

low_findings.title: Local Product Context File Read
low_findings.description: Verdict: TRUE_POSITIVE low-risk filesystem access. The skill asks agents to read product marketing context files when present. This is useful for video planning, but users should understand that local project context may be used in generated outputs.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The instruction explicitly names local context files to read. It is limited to documentation context and does not include hidden paths, traversal, or exfiltration behavior.

low_findings.title: External Service References
low_findings.description: Verdict: TRUE_POSITIVE low-risk network indicator. The skill links to pricing and documentation pages for video tools. These are ordinary reference links and do not perform automatic network requests.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visible markdown references to known video tool sites. I found no code that fetches data or transmits local content to those URLs.

low_findings.title: Relative Documentation Links Flagged As Traversal
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE for path traversal. The ../../ paths are markdown links to integration guides, not file operations. They do not read, write, or escape directories at runtime.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The only traversal-like strings appear inside a markdown table as links. There is no executable code path using these strings for filesystem access.

low_findings.title: Static Text Matches For Crypto And Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The weak cryptography and system reconnaissance alerts match ordinary words, tool names, markdown content, and eval text. No cryptographic implementation, credential handling, system inventory, or reconnaissance behavior is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Review of the cited lines shows prose, model names, eval expectations, and markdown examples. I found no hash function calls, encryption code, or system enumeration instructions.

low_findings.title: Command Examples In Documentation
low_findings.description: Verdict: NEEDS_REVIEW low-risk command guidance. The skill includes npm and npx examples for video tooling. They are legitimate setup examples, but agents should ask before installing packages or running project commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are visible package setup examples and not hidden execution logic. Risk depends on whether an agent executes them without user approval in a real workspace.

content.value_statement: Video production choices can be slow and fragmented across many tools. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code plan practical AI video workflows, prompts, and production steps.
actual_capabilities: Selects between programmatic video, AI generation, avatars, and editing workflows.
Drafts structured prompts for AI video generation models.
Compares Hyperframes and Remotion for template-driven video production.
Plans AI avatar workflows for explainers, updates, and outreach videos.
Outlines repurposing workflows for webinars, podcasts, and long-form recordings.
Recommends aspect ratios and production steps for common video platforms.
limitations: It does not render video by itself without connected tools or local frameworks.
Tool pricing and model availability may change after the skill was written.
It cannot guarantee accurate AI-generated text, logos, or product interfaces inside video footage.
It relies on user-provided brand assets, scripts, and product context for precise output.

use_cases.title: Launch a Product Demo
use_cases.description: Plan a homepage or sales video using screen recordings, overlays, captions, and optional AI-generated B-roll.

use_cases.title: Create Weekly Update Videos
use_cases.description: Build a repeatable avatar or voiceover workflow for regular product announcements without filming every week.

use_cases.title: Repurpose Long-Form Content
use_cases.description: Turn webinars, podcasts, or demos into short social clips with captions and platform-specific formats.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a Video Approach
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me choose the best production approach for a video. The goal is [goal], the platform is [platform], the length is [length], and my assets are [assets].

prompt_templates.title: Draft an AI Video Prompt
prompt_templates.prompt: Create three AI video prompts for [scene]. Include subject, action, camera movement, visual style, lighting, mood, and technical specs.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Programmatic Video Template
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a repeatable programmatic video workflow for [video type]. Compare Hyperframes and Remotion, then recommend the better option for my stack: [stack].

prompt_templates.title: Build a Multi-Tool Production Pipeline
prompt_templates.prompt: Plan an end-to-end video pipeline for [campaign]. Include scripting, avatar or voiceover, generated B-roll, overlays, captions, review steps, and final exports.

output_examples.input: We need a two-minute SaaS product demo for our homepage.
output_examples.output: A production plan with script priorities, real screen recording steps, programmatic overlays, optional B-roll, captions, and a 16:9 export path.

output_examples.input: Create a short AI video prompt for a coffee shop laptop scene.
output_examples.output: A concise visual prompt that defines the subject, action, camera movement, lighting, style, mood, and resolution.

output_examples.input: Turn a webinar into social clips.
output_examples.output: A repurposing workflow that cleans the transcript, extracts strong moments, adds captions, adapts aspect ratios, and prepares distribution.

best_practices: Start with the video goal and platform before selecting tools.
Use real screen recordings for product interfaces and AI footage for supporting visuals.
Add captions and choose aspect ratios before final export.
anti_patterns: Do not ask video models to render precise product UI, readable text, or logos.
Do not generate large batches before reviewing a small pilot sample.
Do not choose an avatar workflow when authentic founder footage is more important than scale.

faq.question: Does this skill create finished video files?
faq.answer: No. It plans workflows and prompts. Rendering requires tools such as Hyperframes, Remotion, HeyGen, Runway, or other video services.

faq.question: Can it help with AI avatar videos?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains when to use avatar tools and how to combine scripts, captions, overlays, and review steps.

faq.question: Can it write prompts for video generation models?
faq.answer: Yes. It structures prompts around subject, action, camera movement, visual style, lighting, mood, and technical details.

faq.question: Is it useful for batch video production?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers template-based workflows for recurring updates, personalized outreach, and data-driven video variants.

faq.question: Does it require API keys?
faq.answer: Only if you use external services that require them. The skill itself is guidance and does not include credentials.

faq.question: What should users verify before publishing?
faq.answer: Review generated footage, captions, product claims, brand assets, permissions, pricing, and platform export settings.
